changes: Strategy & Positioning section: ported three new audited sections from the brand bible into #strategy as standalone canon-cards — #competitive (§3 Competitive Landscape: direct/indirect competitors, positioning map, white space, category conventions, points of parity/difference), #mission-vision (§4 Mission & Vision statements), and #values (§5 Brand Values: five operating values × {Definition, Trade-off Pair, Decision Scenarios})
Each new card carries its own anchor id for direct linking and its own canon-links row back to the bible markdown source

styles.css: added .canon-card h4 rule (purple-deep, uppercase, 14px) for sub-block headings inside the new cards — needed by the dense Competitive Landscape and Values cards where h3 alone wasn't enough hierarchy

Brand-docs cleanup Batch 1 (refactor/brand-docs-cleanup worktree). Step 1 Inventory Delta and Step 2 pre-port decisions are in docs/plans/2026-05-15-brand-docs-cleanup.md. Subsequent batches will port §6 Personality, §7 Mantra, §8 Customer-Relationship extension, §10 Proof Points, §11 Messaging Hierarchy, the Voice Codex R1 keeper, R2 ban-list, and Appendix A Common Decisions.
Color section: rebuilt around the v0.7 surface-system — three-tier token architecture (raw → semantic → component), 11 hues × 4-step ramps, six usage rules tested across eight digital media
Two-register framing added (R2 storefront chrome vs R1 in-universe canon) — heads off the v1 'tasteful editorial' trap where R1 deadpan rules got mis-applied to R2 storefront

New semantic tokens: --surface-input + --surface-overlay (always white — typed-text legibility, modal lift), --surface-chrome-deep (purple-700 for sustained chrome like sidebars/dashboards), --text-running-on-chrome (cream for multi-line on purple — lime stays for short labels only)
Cream is the GROUND; white is what LIFTS off it (cards, inputs, modals) — explicitly documented after A/B/C cream-vs-white test made the difference visible

State-success retuned from #1B6B2A → #1F8B3D — stays green, but at saturation/lightness sibling-pair to state-error red (#C92A2A) so positive/negative state colors feel like the same family. Brand-lime vs state-green ambiguity is handled by hue-family distance (135° vs 85°) plus rule 6 (keep apart in dense data) rather than by hue swap.

Wordmark canon update: the orange-fill wordmark's stacked-shadow stack is now orange (fill) · ink (4px) · lime-400 (8px) — matches the W monogram color stack. Previous canon used orange-deep as the back-shadow; .type-wordmark specimens on brand-guidelines + brand-one-pager updated. Lime-700 and orange-700 role descriptions updated accordingly (deep / structural rather than wordmark-specific). masthead-full.svg regenerated to use the lime back-shadow.

Big Shoulders Display italic correction: BSD is rendered at weight 900 (regular), with slant from CSS skewX(...), not from the italic variant. Prior canon said '900 italic'; that was layering the typeface italic ON TOP of the CSS skew, producing ~16° of compounded slant. All CSS rules using --font-display had font-style: italic removed; specimens + scale tables + bible §4D Typography updated.

Typography system expanded from four to five faces: Bowlby One added as --font-pulp — the secondary 'pulp display' face for hero banners, marquee strips, and ad headlines. One use per page max. Same stacked-shadow treatment as the wordmark (orange/ink/lime), skewX(-7deg). Bowlby One was proven in the v18-the-pulp prototype as a poster sibling to Big Shoulders Display. NEVER 3 expanded to include Bowlby One (not for the wordmark); NEVER 17 added (one-use-per-page rule).
Interactive states now documented for primary / secondary / accent CTAs (default / hover / active / focus / disabled) with full token chain

Contrast ratios now computed live from the :root token values (8 surfaces × 8 text colors matrix + per-pairing badges) — replaces the hand-maintained v0.6 table, so any future palette tweak surfaces here automatically
Backwards-compat: existing --color-purple / --color-lime / etc. aliases preserved, no breaking changes to live components

Body face swapped from Inter to Public Sans (US Web Design System, civic-tech humanist). Rationale: the brand's strongest joke is Winston pretending to run a legitimate authorized importer — mono CYCLE 247 tags, regulatory microline, customs paperwork aesthetic — and Public Sans (literally designed by the federal government for civic websites) extends that deadpan-bureaucratic register into body type itself. Body voice now reads as 'we have our paperwork in order.' Comparison page at /body-face-comparison.html documents the seven-face shortlist. Inter retained as a fallback during font load only. tokens.css + brand-guidelines + brand-one-pager + brand-bible §4D Typography all updated.

Color section: aligned with bible Color canon-flip (2026-05-11) — bible's new 'Visual Identity — Color (Canonical · v2 palette)' section is now the canonical 11-token list with cross-medium values + shadow-stack pairings

Color section: added 'Depth & utility tokens' subsection — Purple Mid #5C27A3 (cosmic gradient mid-stop), Lime Deep #7CB342, Orange Deep #E55A1A (orange-fill wordmark + monogram-primary back-shadow), White #FFFFFF (accent only, never brand ground)
Color section: Why-paragraph clarifies the depth tokens' role inside gradients and the locked shadow stack (4px ink, 8px orange-deep for orange-fill on dark)
Notes: page substance was already on the v2 palette via tokens.css (lime in, teal out, ink #1A0A2E) — this release surfaces the full token set in the swatch grid for completeness

Logo section: real SVG/PNG assets wired in (wordmark-orange-on-cream + wordmark-orange-on-dark + monogram-favicon variants) — placeholder W mark and 'logo is a placeholder' notice removed; logo is now production-ready

Logo section: new 'Logo typeface' card added — documents Lilita One as THE WORDMARK FACE, all-caps casing lock, locked treatment, license info (SIL OFL via Google Fonts), Phase A.7 vibe rationale, and acknowledged Debt 1 (custom-lettering scheduled)
Logo section: clear-space rule updated from 'height of the W' to '1/3 the cap-height of the wordmark' — wordmark is now all-caps so there's no lowercase reference
Favicon block: rebuilt around the real monogram-favicon SVG + 16x16 PNG; the 16px sample uses the native PNG raster for pixel-snap clarity
Typography section: v2 type-role assignments corrected — Wordmark · Lilita One (was mislabelled 'Accent'), Display · Big Shoulders Display 900 italic, Body · Inter, Mono · JetBrains Mono (new row)
Typography Display sample swapped from 'WITTYVERSE LOUD & CRISP' to 'DELIVERED FROM CYCLE 247' — WITTYVERSE is wordmark content (Lilita One), display copy needs different sample text
Typography type scale: added Wordmark / Kicker / Mono tag rows; clarified that Hero/H2/H3 etc. use Big Shoulders Display, not Lilita One

Typography Do/Don't list expanded with NEVER rules from incorrect-usage.md: NEVER 15 (no mixed-case wordmark), NEVER 3 (no Big Shoulders for wordmark), NEVER 4 (skew range), plus Lilita-only-for-wordmark discipline
Font load: added Big Shoulders Display italic (1,700/1,800/1,900) and JetBrains Mono (400/500/600/700) — were missing
Subheading (h3) color on cream cards changed from lime to purple-deep — lime-on-cream failed WCAG contrast; h3 stays lime on dark visual-system sections (Logo, Color, Typography, etc.) where it works
Tightened spacing of h3 sub-sections on cream cards — 32px gap above each h3 (when it follows another element) and 20px below, so sub-sections read as distinct rather than mashed together
Customer section now ships real content from the audited brand bible §2 (Tier 1/Tier 2 profile, Decision Drivers, Decision Journey, Anti-Persona)
Removed placeholder banner from Customer; section is no longer marked as a stub
Added cross-link to the new brand-bible page under Customer canon links
Strategy & Positioning section now ships real content from the audited brand bible §1 (Positioning, Onliness, Why This Position, Sacrifices, Category Frame)
Removed placeholder banner from Strategy & Positioning; section is no longer marked as a stub
Added link to brand-bible.md (audited 2026-05-08) under Strategy canon links
Added sticky left side-nav with scroll-spy (8 top-level + 6 sub-sections under Visual System)
Added click-to-expand overlay viewer for the 6 Surface mockups, with compare-lab toggles available inside the overlay
Cycle between surfaces with ← / → arrows or on-screen chevrons; ESC / backdrop-click to close
Layout extended to 1440px on desktop to accommodate the rail; mobile (<720px) unchanged
Initial brand-guidelines page scaffold + canon visual language
Visual system documented: full color palette + WCAG contrast ratios, type scale, spacing scale, layout grid
Logo + Avatar/Small-Size Mark coverage at 16/32/48/96px
6 surface mockups (homepage hero, product card, footer, IG, X, YouTube) — each demonstrates a different canon pairing
Compare Lab toggle infrastructure wired (URL state, draft badge); ready for v5.1 voice draft when added
Strategy, Customer, Voice, Imagery, Iconography ship as clearly-marked placeholders — to be drafted later
Asset gallery with 8 existing Wittyverse explorations
Discoverable from main wittyverse index (Brand Reference card)
